The options provided are all identical, containing exactly the same set of parameters: TIBC, ferritin, and transferrin saturation. Since iron deficiency anemia is characterized by low ferritin, elevated TIBC, and decreased transferrin saturation—all of which are included across every option—the content within each choice is factually accurate. However, since no distinction exists between them beyond labeling (which may indicate a technical error in presentation), selecting any one represents a correct identification of essential biomarkers involved in diagnosis. Therefore, despite redundancy, the inclusion of all three critical indicators remains appropriate.


Final Answer: A
